lines.en_lit: as all of impurity by corpses the public, the Merciful One permits

Rav Sheshet said: From where do I say as it was taught was standing and sacrificing the omer meal-offering and it became impure in his hand, says bring another in its stead. And if the only there, the say to him: Be shrewd and keep silent;
In any case, it is teaching that he says bring another in its place. Rav Naḥman said: I concede where there are remnants for eating

raises an objection was sacrificing the meal-offering bulls, rams, or sheep, and became impure in his hand, says and bring another - in its stead. And if the only there, the say to him: Be shrewd and keep silent;
What, is it not bulls, rams, and sheep the festival

Rav Naḥman said to you: No, bulls bull idolatry Although communal since it has no time fixed we seek out the rams ram of Aaron Although it has a time fixed since by an individual, we seek out The sheep the sheep that accompanies the omer there are remnants for eating.
raises an objection blood that became impure and sprinkled it unwittingly, accepted. intentionally, not accepted. When that was taught, individual,

Come hear For what does the frontplate effect acceptance? for the blood, for the flesh, and for the fat that became impure whether unwittingly or whether intentionally, whether due to beyond his control or whether willfully, whether in an individual or whether it was in a communal And if it enters your mind impurity is permitted in the public, why do I effect acceptance?
Rav Naḥman said to you: When teaches the frontplate effects acceptance an individual if you wish, say Even you say communal those lack a fixed time.

raises an objection. “And Aaron will gain forgiveness for the sin committed in the sacred things And for which sin does gain forgiveness? If the sin of piggul, it has already been stated: it shall not be accepted” And if it is the sin of notar, it has already been stated: neither shall it be credited
